---
title: "Abdul Baari Bakpa"
about:
  template: trestles
  image: profile.jpg
  image-shape: round
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:abdulbaari.bakpa@sciencespo.fr
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/abdul-baari-bakpa/
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/abdulbaaribakpa
    - icon: file-earmark-pdf
      text: CV
      href: cv.pdf
---

## Welcome

I am a graduate student in the **MRes in Economics** at **Sciences Po, Paris**, where I am supervised by **Prof. Clément Imbert**. I previously completed a BA in Economics at **Kwame Nkrumah University of Science and Technology (KNUST)** in Ghana.

My research sits at the intersection of **development, environmental, and trade economics**, with an empirical focus on Sub-Saharan Africa. I am particularly interested in how infrastructure, market integration, and public policy shape land use, deforestation, agricultural production, and structural transformation.

My ongoing master's thesis, *Market Access and Forest Loss in Ghana*, studies how road-induced changes in market access affect forest loss. The project combines a structural trade framework with satellite data, road-network travel times, and a district-year panel for Ghana from 2001 to 2021. Using an instrumental-variable strategy, I examine whether improved connectivity increases pressure to clear land or instead supports agricultural intensification on existing farmland. Preliminary results suggest that better market access reduces forest loss, partly by increasing the use of modern agricultural inputs rather than expanding cultivated area.

## Research Interests

- Development economics
- Environmental and natural-resource economics
- Public and political economics

## News

- **Apr 2026** — Working on the job-market version of *The Price of Connection: Market Access and Forest Loss in Ghana*.
- **2025** — Co-authored paper *"Environmental Pollution Training and Firms' Actions"* submitted for review.
- **Aug 2024** — Started the MRes in Economics at Sciences Po as a Mastercard Foundation Scholar.
